response_body_utf8: {"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,8]],"date-time":"2026-05-08T15:14:43Z","timestamp":1778253283154,"version":"3.51.4"},"posted":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"group-title":"SSRN","reference-count":0,"publisher":"Elsevier BV","content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":[],"abstract":"<jats:p><p><span>[Revision note: This paper substantially revises a prior submission. The empirical scope has been expanded from 263 institutions in earlier scanner eras to a 543-institution production cohort under v13.1.0 (locked at SHA256 e5250de8e9de07d6), with 95,876 agents and 626,390 governance edges across 66 countries. The methodological framework has been formalised against classical measurement theory (Cronbach-Meehl 1955, Messick 1995, Cronbach et al. 1972, Brennan 2001), and references have been expanded to include direct engagement with the regulatory literature (BCBS, BIS, FSB, Bank of England, FCA, EBA, EIOPA, Federal Reserve, OCC, PRA). The paper has also been deposited at Zenodo (https:\/\/doi.org\/10.5281\/zenodo.19890627) on 29 April 2026.]<\/span><\/p>\n<p>This paper reports the validation of an external measurement instrument for AI governance across regulated financial institutions. The instrument extracts governance topology from public evidence using large language models supplemented by web-scale search and a transparent three-tier evidence classification (Observed, Derived, Inferred). The paper documents a three-era scanner lineage extended by a v13 corrective iteration locked at SHA256 e5250de8e9de07d6, with cryptographic hashes at every pipeline stage, a four-source variance attribution framework, a capture-replay validation protocol, and a three-level cross-version comparability framework grounded in classical measurement theory (Cronbach and Meehl, 1955; Messick, 1995; Cronbach, Gleser, Nanda and Rajaratnam, 1972).<\/p>\n<p>Empirical validation is drawn from a population-scale campaign of 543 regulated institutions covering 95,876 AI agents and 626,390 governance edges across 66 countries. Three findings bear on instrument validity. First, total edges per agent remain stable at 6.47 to 6.58 across eleven independent founding-cohort batches with the expansion cohort at 6.42, demonstrating architectural envelope stability across cohort boundaries. Second, sector-level mean governance scores track the theoretical gradient of prudential supervisory maturity from pension funds (12.10) to banks (20.92), with no sector input during scoring, providing known-groups validity per Cronbach and Meehl (1955). Third, head-to-head comparison of v11.1 and v13.1.0 on 505 rescanned institutions shows v13.1.0 recovers 21.7 percent more observed edges in absolute terms while additionally capturing structural inference that v11.1 did not recover.<\/p><\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.2139\/ssrn.6675603","type":"posted-content","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,8]],"date-time":"2026-05-08T14:55:12Z","timestamp":1778252112000},"source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["The Stationary Sea Measurement Instrument Validation for External Assessment of AI Governance in Regulated Financial Institutions"],"prefix":"10.2139","author":[{"ORCID":"https:\/\/orcid.org\/0009-0008-0424-6119","authenticated-orcid":true,"given":"William","family":"Collins","sequence":"first","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"78","container-title":[],"original-title":[],"deposited":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,8]],"date-time":"2026-05-08T14:55:12Z","timestamp":1778252112000},"score":1,"resource":{"primary":{"URL":"https:\/\/www.ssrn.com\/abstract=6675603"}},"subtitle":[],"short-title":[],"issued":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"references-count":0,"URL":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.2139\/ssrn.6675603","relation":{},"subject":[],"published":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"subtype":"preprint"}}
